title: Coprosmanthus peduncularis
intro: Coprosmanthus peduncularis, also known as Thai teak or Thai coprosma, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Coprosmanthus peduncularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat various health issues in Asian cultures.
